name: Gwydion
description: Gwydion fab Dôn is a prominent figure in Welsh mythology, a skilled magician, warrior, and trickster. He is the son of the goddess Dôn and brother of Arianrhod. Gwydion is renowned for his cunning and shapeshifting abilities, often using illusion and deception to achieve his goals. He is particularly known for his role in the birth of Lleu Llaw Gyffes, whom he raised and protected after Arianrhod cursed him. Gwydion's resourcefulness is evident in his creation of Lleu's name and arms, circumventing Arianrhod's curses. He is also associated with the creation of the 'phantom army' to aid Pryderi in battle and is a master storyteller and poet. Gwydion's actions are often driven by a desire to protect his family and maintain the balance of power, even if it means resorting to trickery. He represents intelligence, adaptability, and the power of magic in the Mabinogion.
subtitle: Master of Magic and Illusion
achievements: Winning Arms and a Name for Lleu

keyMyths.title: The Birth of Lleu Llaw Gyffes
keyMyths.description: Gwydion skillfully manipulated Arianrhod into accidentally naming and arming Lleu, thus fulfilling the conditions of her curses and allowing him to grow into a powerful warrior. This act demonstrates Gwydion's cunning and dedication to his nephew's well-being.

keyMyths.title: The Creation of the Phantom Army
keyMyths.description: Gwydion created a phantom army from toadstools to aid Pryderi in battle, using his magical abilities to deceive the enemy and gain a strategic advantage. This illustrates his mastery of illusion and his willingness to use unconventional tactics.

keyMyths.title: Gwydion's Shapeshifting
keyMyths.description: Gwydion frequently uses his ability to shapeshift to disguise himself and others, allowing him to navigate difficult situations and achieve his goals through deception. This highlights his adaptability and mastery of magical arts.

sources.author: Lady Charlotte Guest
sources.description: A collection of early Welsh tales, including the Fourth Branch, which features Gwydion prominently.
sources.text: A collection of early Welsh tales, including the Fourth Branch, which features Gwydion prominently.
